page: Microsoft Azure FX12-6mds_v2 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the FXmdsv2 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 3 physical cores and 2 threads per core, yielding 6 vCPUs. The processor operates at a high base clock speed of 4.2 GHz and includes a substantial 260 MB L3 cache. This instance is equipped with 252.0 GB of RAM, providing an exceptionally high memory-to-core ratio of 42.0 GB per core. Additionally, it includes 945 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage.

Performance benchmarks reveal strong single-core capabilities, with Geekbench single-core and text processing scores placing in the top 10% tier. Single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression also show strong performance in the top 25% tier. Multi-core performance, including stress-ng and Geekbench multi-core tests, falls into the average range due to the lower core count of 6 vCPUs. Large-block memory bandwidth reaches 51.24 GB/sec for read operations and 36.64 GB/sec for write operations. In LLM inference tests, the server achieves a strong 285.8 tokens/sec for text generation on a small 135M model, but drops to a weak 6.68 tokens/sec on a medium 7B model and 5.95 tokens/sec for prompt processing on a large 70B model.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the FX12-6mds_v2 offers high resource density for memory-bound workloads, allowing users to access massive memory capacity without paying for unnecessary vCPUs. However, the tradeoff is limited parallel compute scaling, as reflected in its average multi-core benchmark results. This hardware profile is highly suited for memory-intensive applications, database operations, caching layers, and single-threaded data processing tasks that require high clock speeds and a large memory footprint but do not require massive multi-threaded CPU scaling or GPU acceleration.
description: Microsoft Azure FX12-6mds_v2 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 6 vCPUs and 252.0 GB of RAM, delivering an exceptional 42.0 GB of memory per core. Powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz with a 260 MB L3 cache, this x86_64 instance excels in single-core performance, with Geekbench single-core and text processing scores ranking in the top 10% tier. The server is equipped with 945 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage, though it lacks a GPU. While multi-core benchmarks and large-model LLM inference speeds are average to weak due to the 6-vCPU limit, its high memory density and fast clock speed make it highly cost-efficient for memory-bound workloads. It is best suited for databases, caching layers, and single-threaded data processing.
og_description: Microsoft Azure FX12-6mds_v2 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 6 vCPUs, 252 GB of RAM, and a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon processor, ideal for memory-intensive databases and caching.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure FX12-6mds_v2 is a memory-optimized VM with 6 vCPUs, 252 GB RAM, and a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon CPU, built for database workloads.
tagline: A high-frequency, memory-dense virtual machine featuring exceptional single-core performance and substantial local storage for memory-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor with a high 4.2 GHz clock speed
Massive 252.0 GB of RAM providing 42.0 GB of memory per core
Top-tier single-core performance in Geekbench and text processing benchmarks
945 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Optimized for memory-intensive databases, caching, and single-threaded workloads
